spec_match.rationale: Builds DCQL queries for openid4vp and org.iso.mdoc requests as the spec defines. The presentation half (navigator.credentials.get) was already in Chrome before v143 — the v143 addition is issuance. The page acknowledges this in its lede ('issuance is the new bit … only makes sense paired with presentation'), so it's framed correctly as supporting context rather than the new feature.

interactive.rationale: Use-case dropdown (age / KYC / education / employment), protocol dropdown, DCQL preview, run button, step-by-step visual flow. 4 controls.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Index promises 'pick a use case … watch the page build the DCQL query, invoke navigator.credentials.get({ digital }), and show the actual rejection or returned presentation.' Page delivers.

edge_cases.rationale: Handles missing credentials.get; falls back to a friendly note when mDL isn't applicable for a use case. Doesn't probe the chrome://flags state, doesn't show what age_over_18 selective disclosure actually returns when it succeeds.

references.rationale: See-also includes https://chromestatus.com/feature/5099333963874304, the WICG spec, and the OpenID4VP spec.

design_system.rationale: Variables-only; step states (ok/bad/pending) use accent tokens.

openQuestions.title: Add the chrome://flags banner
openQuestions.detail: Same as the other two concepts in this feature. The 'Run' button will always error without the flag. CLAUDE.md rule #7.

openQuestions.title: Show a successful presentation payload
openQuestions.detail: When the API resolves with a credential, the page logs `protocol=...` and partial data. Selective disclosure is the whole point of DCQL — show what the returned VP looks like (jwt header.payload.signature, mDL CBOR) so visitors learn what an actual presentation contains.

openQuestions.title: Add an attestation-replay-attack note
openQuestions.detail: DCQL queries include a `nonce` that protects against replay. The page generates `crypto.randomUUID()` per call but never explains why. A small annotation when the nonce field is highlighted would surface a security best-practice that's not obvious.

openQuestions.title: Cross-link to the v141 / v149 presentation-support concept pages
openQuestions.detail: Digital credentials presentation has its own chromestatus / feature pages in earlier and later releases. Linking out would let visitors compare the presentation half over time — and explain why this concept is supporting context not the headline.

summary: Well-framed supporting concept that explicitly acknowledges presentation pre-dates v143. Same blocker as the other two: no flag-enable banner so the Run button always errors. Also worth showing a successful VP payload and explaining the nonce.
